## Still open

## PR #30 review feedback disposition ledger

### Accepted and remediated

#### Source query-key collision — Codex (`PRRT_kwDOTLXA2c6QUZrM`)

Accepted with modified remediation in `dc74b57`. MathRead state now uses the single namespaced `mathread-view` value; the source URL otherwise remains unchanged.
This preserves source-owned query fields rather than reserving `mrpage`, `mrx`, `mry`, or `mrzoom`.

### Rejected / modified

#### Clipboard rejection handling — Kilo (`PRRT_kwDOTLXA2c6QUZWh`) and Cubic (`PRRT_kwDOTLXA2c6QUego`)

Rejected.
A rejected clipboard write cannot reach either success flash, and the implementation supplies no fallback reader identity.
The requested synthetic permission-denial branch and silent error logging do not advance Issue #9's source-preserving copy contract; the claimed browser proof covers the real successful clipboard boundary.

#### Defensive `goToXY` catch — Kilo (`PRRT_kwDOTLXA2c6QUZnX`)

Rejected.
The copied state is produced by the extension and validated at the reader boundary.
Adding a catch-and-continue path for manually corrupted state would convert an invalid MathRead state into a normal open without proving a user-observable requirement of Issue #9.

#### Fallback for malformed MathRead view state — Kilo (`PRRT_kwDOTLXA2c6QUZnb`) and Cubic (`PRRT_kwDOTLXA2c6QUegk`)

Rejected.
A `mathread-view` value is an all-or-nothing extension-owned state envelope.
Invalid values fail at that boundary; treating a partial or edited envelope as an ordinary source open would add an unclaimed fail-open behavior.
